behavior_surfaces: desktop Settings > Workflows template delete action
state_contracts: A user workflow template delete request opens a confirmation dialog first.
Cancelling the confirmation dialog must not call sessionsApi.deleteWorkflowTemplate.
The existing delete API/storage behavior is called only after the user confirms deletion.

verification.result: RED before production edit: 1 failed because no delete confirmation dialog existed; GREEN after implementation: 48 passed.

known_unknowns: Project cognition entry readiness was review/partial_refresh with path coverage gaps for desktop workflow components; live reads were limited to the returned workflow component paths.
Manual visual browser smoke was not run for this small fast-path change; behavior is covered by Testing Library dialog and API-call assertions.
confidence_notes: Change is frontend-only and limited to the workflow template manager row delete flow.
No workflow template API, registry, router, persistence, built-in workflow creation, or workflow modification semantics were changed.
The destructive-operation consequence gate stood down because the only semantic change is an additional user confirmation before the existing delete mutation.
user_decisions: User requested a confirmation popup for deleting workflow templates from the Workflows settings page.

boundary.scope: sp-fast change limited to adding a confirmation layer before desktop workflow template deletion.
excluded: workflow template storage or migration changes
workflow registry or authoring tool changes
